entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA OP/Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, in general we have got the solution up and running in the S/4HANA On-Premise system. One observation is that the ship-to BP comes from the customer number assignment for the receiving plant when we assigned a storage location which derived a different customer BP on the Shipping tab of the STO. The outbound delivery actually takes the customer BP on the Shipping tab of the STO. This is inconsistent and would cause confusion for the users. Is this standard or considered a bug? I have looked up OSS but could not find any relevant notes. Appreciate any clarification on the above. Cheers.

Siow Chen

entry.title: BPMon monitoring for Number range
entry.content:
• How to put the correct threshold value for yellow and red for the key figure 'Number range intervals with critical fill level'?
  • How the filling level can be calculated? 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BPMon monitoring for Number range
entry.content:
The return value of the Key Figure 'Number range intervals with critical fill level' will be the count of the number range which exceeds the threshold of Filling level if you configured it.
Therefore, the threshold in the parameter should be the number of 'Number range objects' which exceeds the threshold.

Let's say followings are configured in the parameter set.
- Threshold for Yellow: 0
- Threshold for Red: 1

And the filling level is set higher than 90%.

If there is only one Number range object which filling level is over 90%, the key figure will return the count 1. As this return value exceeds the value of 'Threshold for Yellow' but didn't exceeds so the value of 'Threshold for Red', an alert will occur with the yellow rating.

If there are 2 Number range objects which filling level is over 90%, the key figure will return the count 2. As this return value exceeds the value of 'Threshold for Red', an alert will occur with the red rating.

If you felt difficulties when checking the current filling level of the number range objects, I recommend you to use the report 'TBI_REPORTS' via the transaction ST13 as mentioned in the KBA 2363076.
Via this report, you can simulate the key figure to check what kind of values will be returned by the key figure. And when you displayed the detail list from the result, you can also check the corresponding entries of Number ranges with its currently filling level %.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

Have a Good day.

I'm comefrom Asia-Pacific(APAC) and completed the learning journey. But my SAP World just beginng!! I'm ready to grab the certification for stand firmly in SAP World. Please kindly provide the voucher to me

entry.title: Re: No response when clicking close button on fragement i...
entry.content: Update the fragement with the following :

entry.title: Re: No response when clicking close button on fragement i...
entry.content:
Update the fragement with the following: 

<core:FragmentDefinition  xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:l="sap.ui.layout" xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns:u="sap.ui.unified" xmlns:upload="sap.m.upload" >
	<Dialog id="uploadDialogSet" title="File Upload" core:require="{handler: 'freightorderattachmgt/ext/fileHandle/FileHandle'}">
        <content>
            <upload:UploadSet uploadEnabled="true" id="uploadSet" items="{path: '/', templateShareable: false}" fileTypes="png,pdf" maxFileNameLength="200" beforeUploadStarts="handler.onBeforeUploadStart" uploadCompleted="handler.onUploadSetComplete" afterItemRemoved="handler.onItemRemoved"
            terminationEnabled="true">
                <upload:UploadSetItem id="_IDGenUploadSetItem1" visibleRemove="true" visibleEdit="false" fileName="{name}" url="/upload">
                    <upload:attributes>
                        <ObjectAttribute id="_IDGenObjectAttribute1" title="Uploaded by" text="{user}" active="false"/>
                    </upload:attributes>
                </upload:UploadSetItem>
            </upload:UploadSet>
        </content>
        <buttons>        
            <Button id="_IDGenButton1" text="Upload" press="handler.onUploadSet" icon="sap-icon://upload-to-cloud" type="Emphasized"/>
            <Button id="cancel" press="handler.onCloseDialog" text="Cancel" icon="sap-icon://cancel"/>
        </buttons>
	</Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

Then the issue gone .

entry.title: Script layout changes not reflected in print preview
entry.content:
Dear Team,

When ever we try to change with SPACES,TEXT FIELDS,BINDINGS in SAP Script, That layout changes have been reflected from development client to testing client through SCC1N.

Then execute the test print preview in SE71 it's working fine but execution with driver program(Z-Tcode) it's not reflected in print preview/print/spool.

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Script layout changes not reflected in print preview
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

If the print preview in SE71 is working fine,it means the data has been transported to test client correctly.

If in your customized program you are not getting correct print preview,pls help confirm how your program calls the sascript form
and if you are using the same output device in Z program as in the test with SE71.
You can also run report RSTXDELL to delete sapscript load and then test again.

Best Regards

SAP Support

Also when the App/browser is closed, backend session is not released in S4

Anyone faced this issue

Regards

MB

Hello from Australia!

I have recently watched the recording of the International Women's Day online SAP Event and really enjoyed listening to the speakers and the questions which were asked. Well done David, Lin & Sandra!

I have had an SAP Mining career for going close to 17 years now in the Work Management/Accounts Payable/Supply/Technology space (yes wearing the hard hats & steel capped boots) and really do enjoy teaching/coaching others how to use SAP & especially Fiori.

Looking forward to meeting new people & learning new things.

@David_Chaviano @jmmargo

entry.title: Re: The Importance of Public Speaking for Visibility of Own Expertise
entry.content:
I believe that it is very important to overcome the fear of being judged and confiding in one's abilities to take the first step towards public speaking. It really helps in exchanging ideas and building meaningful relationships.

It obviously requires a lot of prep and understanding of the audience we are presenting to and always imporves over time. The idea of presenting to a vast audience still gives me burtterflies inmy stomach but I have learnt to still pursue public speaking despite all odds 🙂

entry.title: Failed to load resource: net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR when open SAPGUI app from Fiori
entry.content:
Good day everyone, we are experiencing issue with SAPGUI in Fiori. Every time we open SAPGUI app from Fiori, it always shows blank and when we checked the error in the console, it always shows Failed to load resources: nett::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR 200. Other Fiori apps are fine, it's the transaction/SAPGUI apps that are affected. 

Below, we tried to open TCode 'BP' from Fiori:

TCODE BP from Fiorierrors in console taberrors in network tab

The tile:

target application fieldstiles

What troubleshooting steps can I take to identify the root cause of this issue? Thank you for your time and attention.

Regards.

Our system: 

  • Fiori Embedded
  • DEV client
  • SAP S/4HANA 2022 SP03 (on premise)
  • SAP FIORI FES 2022 FOR S/4HANA SP03
  • ABAP PLATFORM 2022 SP03
  • SAP S/4HANA FOUNDATION 2022 SP03
  • SAP FIORI FOR SAP S/4HANA 2022 SP03
  • SAPUI5 1.108.22 (202308300810)

entry.title: Re: Unique Constraint Violated Error for Master Data
entry.content:
@indu_sharma 

This happens when you have non unique property value being loaded for an attribute. Just check if there are records where you have more than one attribute value against a property for same master data ID.

 

Br.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: EML to create Sales Order not working
entry.content: After executing the EML in a separate class, I was indeed able to see that a mandatory field is missing during commit. Since, inside the behavior implementation class, commit happens internally, there was no way we could find this error. Thanks anyway for your support.

entry.content:
Hi Team,

Hope you all are doing great,

I am creating GSTR2 Report for Accounts Payable Process in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud. It is a Compliance Report for India. Standard Report is not available so we are creating it by using CDS View app. I am facing Following Errors and unable to proceed further.

  1. I have added Supplier Invoice Data source, while adding Storage Location from Material Document Item data source multiple entries are generating as there is no Data Source available for Line item like Supplier Invoice Item Data Source. Please advise Is there any way to add Storage location so that Multiple entries should not generate.
  2. My client is using Custom Duty Process for which they are booking Custom Duty and GST on Assessable Value but in CDS View, I am unable to get the Assessable Value. Can you Please Suggest if any Data Source is available for Assessable value or How to add the Tax amount calculated on Assessable value. 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Shreshtha Pareek

hi,

my suggestion

use -user exits = template load via tcode mm91

or 

mm tcode=mass

regards,

vijay kalbhor

entry.title: Oracle linux/OS upgrade
entry.content:
Hello team,

ALL our sap systems are on AWS with oracle database and oracle linux 7.x. since support is getting expired this year end, we would like to have some recommendations from you; 

  1. what is the best/recommended approach for upgrade --> in-place or out-of-place/fresh install
  2. Any SAP notes/documentation referring to oracle linux upgrade?
  3. Do you recommend any technical procedure for the same. 

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Oracle linux/OS upgrade
entry.content:
In regards to your request for recommendations, it generally depends on your company's needs, available resources, downtime allowance and expertise.

  1. In-Place vs Out-of-Place Upgrade: These are the two main approaches available for your situation. An in-place upgrade involves updating the current system that your SAP systems are running on, while an out-of-place upgrade indicates installing a new version of the system and migrating your SAP applications to that version.

    • In-Place: This would result in less work overall since you are not reinstalling everything from scratch, but it could come with risks as things might not go as planned which may impact your system uptime.

    • Out-of-Place: This approach would be somehow safer as you can parallelly run the new system while keeping the old one operational, providing you with a better rollback plan if issues arise. However, it requires more resources & effort as it involves setting up new systems and migrating existing applications.

  2. please refer to note 2069760 - Oracle Linux 7.x SAP Installation and Upgrade

  3. ghly recommended to involve your IT together with Oracle support during the planning and execution of the upgrade. Ensure that you have a proper backup and recovery strategy in place as well as thorough testing procedures to verify that all your SAP applications function as expected after the upgrade. You should also plan for necessary employee training for any functional changes from OS upgrade.

Remember, ultimately the final decision depends on your specific circumstance and should be done based on thorough planning, risk analysis and testing.

entry.title: MIGO ERROR: The system could not create any outputs
entry.content:
Hi Friends,

If I click the individual slip check box, Then it gives the message "The system could not create any outputs". output will not trigger. I have checked in debugging mode, always xnast is empty, so it gives the message.

 

Thanks with regards,

Vallamuthu M.

entry.title: 'INTO' is invalid (due to grammar) error while SQL in BAPI
entry.content:
Hi guys!

The error occurs in the first INTO of the first SELECT. I have 'INTO' is invalid (due to grammar)
I need to return several tables that will become arrays in an application that will call this BAPI remotely (RFC). However, when I add this code to the SAP that will run the RFC, it gives a problem with the INTO clause.
INTO is invalid here (due to grammar), but I don't know what I'm doing wrong, because I've already run this function in other SAPs and it worked normally.
The logic of this RFC is to return the data from the MARD, MARC, MAKT and MBEW tables based on the MARA table. Then I perform a select with where in the MARA table that will return a series of items, and based on the code of these items I perform a FOR ALL ENTRIES to search for these same materials in other tables and return them separately. I can't use joins. And then I come across the INTO clause error when I use anything between the word MARA (FROM MARA) and INTO. (In this case it's the order by)
I need the up to to be able to limit the lines of this return.
Can anyone help me?

Code (also attached): 

FUNCTION Z_GET_MATERIAL_FULL_CUSTOM.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*" IMPORTING
*" REFERENCE(MARA_MAX_ROWS) TYPE INT4
*" REFERENCE(MARA_SKIP_ROWS) TYPE INT4
*" TABLES
*" MARA_TABLE STRUCTURE MARA
*" MARD_TABLE STRUCTURE MARD
*" MARC_TABLE STRUCTURE MARC
*" MAKT_TABLE STRUCTURE MAKT
*" MBEW_TABLE STRUCTURE MBEW
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
SELECT MATNR, MEINS, MTPOS_MARA FROM MARA WHERE MTPOS_MARA = 'ZMAP' OR MTPOS_MARA = 'ZMAN' ORDER BY MATNR INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @Mara_TABLE UP TO @Mara_MAX_ROWS ROWS OFFSET @Mara_SKIP_ROWS .

SELECT MATNR, LABST, LGPBE FROM MARD FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @Mara_TABLE WHERE MATNR = @Mara_TABLE-MATNR INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @MARD_TABLE.


SELECT MATNR, MINBE, MABST, PLIFZ, DISMM, STEUC FROM MARC FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @Mara_TABLE WHERE MATNR = @Mara_TABLE-MATNR INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @Marc_TABLE.


SELECT MATNR, MAKTX FROM MAKT FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @Mara_TABLE WHERE MATNR = @Mara_TABLE-MATNR AND SPRAS = 'P' INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @makt_TABLE.


SELECT MATNR, VERPR FROM MBEW FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @Mara_TABLE WHERE MATNR = @Mara_TABLE-MATNR INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @MBEW_TABLE.


ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Synching Folder Structure between SEN Producer and SEN Manager
entry.content:
Hi All,

In SEN Producer, I created a folder structure as shown in the picture below. I created the Content folder structure outside Unsorted. 

SEN Producer Screen with Folder Structure

Next, when I opened the SEN Manager and done the refresh, the Content folder structure created in SEN Producer successfully appears in SEN Manager, but it appears under Unsorted by default, as shown in the picture below.

SEN Manager Screen

My Question: When the SEN Manager is synched with SEN Producer, should the SEN manager show/fetch the folder structure similar to the SEN Producer? In my case, in SEN Producer, the Content folder appears outside Unsorted, but my SEN Manager shows the Content folder inside Unsorted when synched.

entry.title: Re: Handling of bank charges in bank statement
entry.content: Hi Bohdan, many thanks for preparing this. I've tried to replicate this in our system but the posting area 2 still doesn't post the bank charges but the debit and credit was already deducted the bank charges are maintained in FEBEP-SPESK. I found in other blogs that Posting type for the posting rule of posting area 2 must be 7 or 8 , clearing the subledger. Is there any other possible way? Thanks in advance.

Phaneendra

entry.title: SAP Build IRPA not recognize screen on virtual machine
entry.content:
Hello, 

we have a virtual machine where we have out unattended agent. I have created an automation on my personal computer and it is working fine there. Unfortunately the desktop agent at the virtual machine is not recognizing the second screen of the automation. When I loaded traces, it looks like that the agent is not even aware of that the screen is loaded (see screenshots, there is no information about screen loaded but when I start it on my PC, the screen is loaded). I can see that the screen is loaded at the virtual machine, but the agent did not cach it. Do you have any ideas why is this happening?

Regards,

Preeti

entry.title: Re: Migration cockpit in fiori
entry.content:
Yes, in Migration Cockpit you can simulate the migration process before running the data upload so that you can detect the errors in advance. Please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/da6a3a95ee7d44ea8845bbfc3889a318.html?q=migration%20cockpit&locale=en-US

Thanks.

Auto. assgmnt creation for demand 0000XXXXXXXXX / 0010 failed; partner is not available

entry.title: Introducing the SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the launch of the SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal - your one-stop destination for all learning offerings related to SAP Cloud ALM. With a comprehensive range of learning materials, structured learning paths, and additional resources, this platform provides an exceptional learning experience to help individuals succeed in their roles. Let's explore what this exciting new platform has to offer.

Featured Learning Offering: Strategic Topics with SAP Cloud ALM

The SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal kicks off with its latest featured learning offerings, regularly updated to provide the most popular and relevant opportunities. Currently, there are two featured courses:

  • Solution Transformation Consultant Training

This course is designed for consultants responsible for driving business transformation using an end-to-end tool chain. It covers the design-to-deploy phase, including SAP Cloud ALM and Tricentis Test Automation for SAP.

  • SAP Cloud ALM Implementation and Operations Configuration Webinar Series

This webinar series helps users efficiently manage projects, optimize processes, orchestrate tests, and gain insights through analytics. Recorded sessions are available for replay.

SAP Cloud ALM Learning Journeys: Learn at Your Own Pace

Our learning journeys on learning.sap.com offer a structured pathway to master SAP Cloud ALM at your preferred pace. These journeys are designed to cater to diverse learning preferences while ensuring flexibility and convenience. Whether you're new to SAP Cloud ALM or seeking to enhance your existing skills, our personalized learning paths are tailored to meet your needs and goals.

Additional Learning Resources: Expanding Your Knowledge

This section provides additional opportunities to deepen your understanding of SAP Cloud ALM. It includes:

  • Learning Offerings for Supported Solutions

If you have a specific solution focus, check out this part which directs you to the dedicated content for your SAP products. You'll also find information on how SAP Cloud ALM supports the Activate Methodology and CLEAN CORE.

  • How-to and deep-dive learning materials

For those seeking hands-on learning as an expert, this section collects expert-level knowledge of SAP Cloud ALM.

  • Continuous Learning Opportunities: Events and Beyond

Stay connected with like-minded professionals, thought leaders, and industry experts by participating in ALM events and other channels that provide valuable content and foster community interactions.

Regardless of whether you are a customer or a partner, the SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal caters to your specific learning needs. It is designed to accommodate individuals at all skill levels, from beginners to advanced users, and offers continuous learning opportunities to help users stay up to date with the latest advancements in SAP Cloud ALM.

Don't miss out on the opportunity to enhance your understanding of SAP Cloud ALM. Take advantage of the SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal today and discover how it can elevate your knowledge and expertise to new heights. Start your learning journey now and unlock the full potential of SAP Cloud ALM.

entry.title: Re: SAP Basis Learning: Work processes
entry.content:
Hello,

If you like this video you can check also one about starting SAP with sapcontrol. It includes an explanation of how the mechanism works with examples and diagrams. Beginners may also find the startup troubleshooting session interesting. Enjoy.

entry.title: The relationship for employee can't be used for opportunity involved parties
entry.content:
Hiii All,

I have one issue please help me.tge customer created a customer party' role and also create an opportunity party role but when  creating a new opportunity the customer party' role is not being added to the opportunity.even leaving the rule with more information it is not being triggered can anyone suggest this issue why it is happening

entry.title: Configurate the connection between S4 OP and Datasphere
entry.content:
Could you provide a step through guide for configurate the connection between S4 OP and Datasphere?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Configurate the connection between S4 OP and Datasphere
entry.content:
Please have a look at below on-line document about connection between S/4HANA On-Premise and SAP Datasphere

Prepare Connectivity to SAP S/4HANA On-Premise | SAP Help Portal

SAP S/4HANA On-Premise Connections | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Fiori
entry.content: how to use Fiori in SAP? and what are the requirements needed?

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
Issue while Inspection Results Recording in DMC
entry.content:
Hi Team,

While doing Inspection Results Recording in CUSTOM_WORKCENTER_POD, I am getting the error "Collaboration execution failed from external error: Defects occured during confirmation;No single results can be recorded for characteristic 0010".

Before this, I added MIC and sampling procedure for In Process inspection for a routing in S4H on-premises. And I saved the result for sample in DMC but getting this error.

BR,

Kiran

SAP Digital Manufacturing SAP Manufacturing Execution @ManoelCosta

entry.title: Re: how to remove authentication and access a fiori app a...
entry.content: thank you. i couldnt access the link . if possible can you please paste xsapp.json here.

entry.title: Field for Training Provider at Item Level
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

We have a requirement to identify the training provider for each of the Item, i.e. Internal Depart 1, External Vendor A etc. I'm wondering if there is an existing standard field for such at Item level? If so, is it free-text for referenced field? If there is no such field, could you share your experience/ best practices in retaining training provider information in SF LMS?

An extended question will be: is there any documentation on the list of standard field available for Item/ Curriculum?

entry.title: Re: Categorization of Products as Articles
entry.content: Thanks @MarkoLange for the clarification.

entry.title: label and remove filters in CAP application
entry.content:
i have already applied labelling to fields but in the search it still shows field id and not the field label.

Also how to remove certain fields from the filter?

entry.title: Append files from 2 different sources using sap cloud integration
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement where there are 3 files (File1.xml, File2.xml , File3.xml) in system A and similar set of files in system B. We have to club these files and sent to target only if system B has all files as system A. Otherwise we should not process them. Is it possible in CI? Please advise

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Fiori
entry.content:
Hi,

Refer to these links

Prerequisites for Implementing SAP Fiori Cloud 

Installation Requirements 

Prerequisites and Restrictions 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Create Permission role to have only view access to Employee Central configuration
entry.content: Very interesting!

entry.title: Re: error w while trying to create a blog
entry.content:
I had the same issue. You will need to finish the tutorial by going to Tour the SAP Community 2024.

I didn't do Step 5 properly (you have to join and leave a comment in a group), and therefore I was still getting the same error saying I didn't have enough privileges. 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Updating Bank master data after Golive with Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
Hello

I found a line in the migration object information help for "Bank" as below. 

Do Not Overwrite Existing Data

This is an indicator field to determine whether or not to overwrite an existing bank.

If the indicator field is empty, the system overwrites an existing bank. If you don’t want to overwrite an existing bank, enter X.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cc9ecc5f5f6b400a98d10e930324f7ad/216ce7ce1cad45a68e2486ca4203da69.html?locale=en-US

It seems we can perform mass change for bank master data in the system. I thought it would be great if we can still use this function on a daily base even after Golive. For example, item field: Bank Groups or text.

Does anyone know if that is possible and have the experience?

Kind Regards,

Eitaro

entry.title: Re: In Outbound POSC WT for OB04 hasn't created
entry.content:
Hi,

OB04 in the standard is a loading step. And it makes sense that this WT is not automatically created when the WT before is confirmed - because then the next resource may just drop the pallet down the door, but before the truck has arrived. You might want to create all the loading WTs once the truck is there. For this you have an extra transaction, where you can create unloading / loading WTs (don't know the TA code now).

Edit: to see how it is setup, you would have to check the customizing for your process.

Brgds

Juergen

---
All the above is no official SAP statement.

Want to learn EWM?
Check for EWM courses: https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Extended+Warehouse+Management-EWM+in+S4HANA
Get a SAP Learning Hub Subscription: https://training.sap.com/learninghub

And it is EWM. NOT eWM - Duh!
And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

entry.title: Re: Error in J_1IG_INV
entry.content:
Hi @0025121074 

The below OSS Note might be helpful

2435112 - GST - STO - Common error in transaction code J_1IG_INV - SAP for Me

Thanks & Regards

Sanil Bhandari

entry.title: Re: Can I use DOX Custom Schemas (w/ AI functionality) wi...
entry.content: Thanks Ivan. The note was exactly my 'blind spot' 🙂

entry.title: Re: output determination
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check whether the billing document has been released to accounting or not, as the Requirement 62 mentioned in ODP requires it.

Regards

RK

entry.title: CPE Hours for Different Professional Qualifications
entry.content:
Sf LMS provides a standard CPE (Continuing Professional Education) Hours field. However, it does not seem to provide a standard field to input what Professional Qualification(s) the Item is applicable to (whether free-text or referenced), i.e. Project Management Professional, Certified Associate in Project Management (CAPM) & Society for Human Resources Management (SHRM) etc. Is this understanding correct?

A possible work around I can think of is to use "Subject" (old) / "Comptency" (new) to tag such learnings. Can anyone advise if there are other ways of differentiating the applicable Professional Qualfication?

Also, in the situation where 1 course is applicable to 2 different professional qualifications, and is recognized for different number of CPE hours (i.e. 1hr CPE for Qualification A and 1.5hr for Qualification B), is there a way manage such differing CPE hours for the same item?

entry.title: WORKFLOW EXPORT FROM QUALITY TO PRODUCTION
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

Is it possible to export workflows that are tested in quality to production (eg:- supplier invoice workflows).

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAP for Two Types of Material Receipt Pro...
entry.content:
Hi,

I think to answer this, it would be necessary to know your organizational structure and what you are doing. And: do you have an SAP system set up already? Or is that an implementation question?

"This warehouse is located near our factory, but between the warehouse and the factory we have high-turnover stock storages to supply the factory."

Is "the warehouse" and "high-turnover stock storages" one warehouse number? Means the stock belongs to the same plant + storage location, which is connected to this warehouse number?

The next question is: 

"For high-turnover/priority processes: Materials are delivered directly to the storages, bypassing the warehouse due to the slow delivery and exit process to the factory.
For medium-turnover/priority processes: Materials are delivered to the warehouse, and after receipt and organization, when requested, they are sent to the storages and consequently to the factory."

How do you identify this? When and how do you know what a "high-turnover/priority process" is? Is it depending on the material? Or that you just need this in that moment?

Brgds

Juergen

---
All the above is no official SAP statement.

Want to learn EWM?
Check for EWM courses: https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Extended+Warehouse+Management-EWM+in+S4HANA
Get a SAP Learning Hub Subscription: https://training.sap.com/learninghub

And it is EWM. NOT eWM - Duh!
And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

entry.title: Optional Surveys will come with H12024 also?
entry.content:
Since in 1H2024 we will have the optional trainings visible on the Learner's home page, will the Optional surveys be also visible?

 

#LMS #SFSFLMS

entry.title: Programs in 1H 2024
entry.content:
Currently, optional and recommended programs are not displayed in the learning plan at all, only mandatory ones.
Will this be solved in 1H, or what is the timeline for this?

 

thank you!
Adina

entry.title: Re: condition type FOR VENDOR
entry.content: Hello Prasoon,

entry.title: Re: Creating multiple PM Malfunction Reports at once
entry.content:
Hi,

You can use copy form option for this or can create program for this purpose. 

regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales Performance Management Best Practices for Better Customer Outcomes
entry.content: hi please share the SAP Sales Performance Management best practice documents

entry.title: Lesson learnt on Process Industry with Production Information System (S4 HANA Custom Development)
entry.content:
PURPOSE:

The idea behind this blog is to highlight some of the custom developments in SAP PP module named as Production Information System implementation for Process Manufacturing Industry Scenario.

REQUIREMENTS:

They wish to keep track and update the Water and Power Production/Consumption data against each Plant and Phase Level (sublevel of Plant).
They will monitor against the Unit level for the boiler, turbine, and desal. Approval will be based on the Actual

SOLUTION:

This is going to be totally customized (custom screen and a table will be developed to meet the business requirement) solution as standard SAP functionalities will not match the requirement. Standard PP functionalities will match to some extent, but to maintain the uniformity of the solution, it is recommended to go with a Tailer Made solution.

The main objective of this project is to facilitate the below functionalities.

  • Screens for Updating the production/consumption details i.e., replica of PIS.
    Key parameters which are needed for calculation and reports.


PIS MASTER DATA:
Outage Types:
This master data table will be used in all the Production Recording screens. Below outage types will be maintained in a custom table and will be used in production screens.

Maintenance, Planned, Un Scheduled Shut Down, Reserved, Overhaul Outage

FORECAST:
A custom table will be used to maintain the forecast values of Water in SAP PIS. Below fields are needed.
Plant, date, Power, Water.

 

MAIN PLANT SCREEN: (Plant entry daily for plant Information)
This screen is needed to update the following information against a plant. Main Plant Date will be in the header level.
Max Power Generation

Max Generation Time

Min Power Generation

Min Generation Time

PHASE LEVEL ENTRY: (Phase entry daily for phase information)
Water / Electricity consumptions will be recorded at Phase Level. Below are the data will be updated in the screen. Plant and Phase will be there in the header section.
Fields details:

Water Consumed for Housing (M3)

Water Consumed Phase Utilities(M3)

Demin Water (M3)

DESAL ENTRY DAILY & OUTAGE HOURS
This is one of the major screens where business will record Production for MSF and MED units. The entry is at Unit level.
Plant and Phase will be there in the header section with date.

Fields details:

Online production – Production of the Multistage Flash (MSF) / Multiple Effect Distillation (MED).
Dump Production – Dump Production of the MSF/MED unit.
Reserve Production – Reserve Production of the MSF/MED unit.
Total Production - Auto Calculated (Online + Dump + Reserve)
Reason per min – The code for the less production. Predefined fields will be given, and business will select accordingly.


OUTAGE REASON SCREEN
This sub screen is needed to record the outage time and reason for the attached unit. Outage Reason screen will be added to all the production units.

              Available hours = Service Hours + Reserve Shutdown Hours
              Service Hours = Running hours of Unit with production
              Forced Outage Hours = Trip Hours + Forced S/D Hours
              Scheduled Outage = Planned Outage Hours + Maintenance Outage Hours + Overhaul Maintenance


RO ENTRY DAILY & OUTAGE HOURS


 Plant and Phase will be there in the header section with date.
Fields details:

On-line production – Production of the RO unit.
Dump Production – Dump Production of the RO unit.
Reserve Production – Reserve Production of the RO unit.
Total Production - Auto Calculated (Online + Dump + Reserve).


TURBINE ENTRY DAILY & OUTAGE HOURS
Plant and Phase will be there in the header section with date.
Fields details:

Gross Power - Power Generated for the TG
Auxiliary consumption - Power Consumption for BTG
Fuel Gas - Fuel Consumption of TG unit (Eastern Region)
Liquid Fuel - Fuel Consumed of TG in (Ton)
Diesel - Diesel Consumed entered in (M3) of TG.


BOILER DATA ENTRY & OUTAGE HOURS
Plant and Phase will be there in the header section with date.
Fields details:

Steam Production – Produced steam of Boiler/HRSG.
Flue Gas Temperature – Temperature of the Fuel Gas of Boiler / HRSG
Combustion Air Flow -   Combustion air flow for Boiler/HRSG


DATA APPROVE (FOR PRODUCTION & PIPELINES)
Computed - Value which is entered in unit level and summed up at plant level.
Entered - Values entered in the plant screen.
City, quantity - Data entered in export water to cities daily against plant against each city.
Total Water Export - Data entered in export water to cities daily against plant.
Total Power Export - Data entered in Phase Screen for export Power against plant Summed up.
Phase Power Export - Data entered in Phase Screen for export Power against Phase
Phase Power Import - Data entered in Phase Screen for Import Power against Phase
Desal / Power / Boiler / RO - Data entered in unit level against the Phase selected.

 

Please follow my profile for future blogs related to SAP Production Planning.

Check out my LinkedIn Profile -> Abishek K G 

Let me know your views and suggestion to make this blog post better.
Thanks for taking your timeout to read this blog post.

Warm Regards,
Abishek K G

entry.title: Lesson learnt on Process Industry with Production Information System (S4 HANA Custom Development)
entry.content:
PURPOSE:

The idea behind this blog is to highlight some of the custom developments in SAP PP module named as Production Information System implementation for Process Manufacturing Industry Scenario.

REQUIREMENTS:

They wish to keep track and update the Water and Power Production/Consumption data against each Plant and Phase Level (sublevel of Plant).
They will monitor against the Unit level for the boiler, turbine, and desal. Approval will be based on the Actual

SOLUTION:

This is going to be totally customized (custom screen and a table will be developed to meet the business requirement) solution as standard SAP functionalities will not match the requirement. Standard PP functionalities will match to some extent, but to maintain the uniformity of the solution, it is recommended to go with a Tailer Made solution.

The main objective of this project is to facilitate the below functionalities.

Screens for Updating the production/consumption details i.e., replica of PIS.
Key parameters which are needed for calculation and reports.


PIS MASTER DATA:
Outage Types:
This master data table will be used in all the Production Recording screens. Below outage types will be maintained in a custom table and will be used in production screens.

Maintenance, Planned, Un Scheduled Shut Down, Reserved, Overhaul Outage

FORECAST:
A custom table will be used to maintain the forecast values of Water in SAP PIS. Below fields are needed.
Plant, date, Power, Water.

 

MAIN PLANT SCREEN: (Plant entry daily for plant Information)
This screen is needed to update the following information against a plant. Main Plant Date will be in the header level.
Max Power Generation

Max Generation Time

Min Power Generation

Min Generation Time

PHASE LEVEL ENTRY: (Phase entry daily for phase information)
Water / Electricity consumptions will be recorded at Phase Level. Below are the data will be updated in the screen. Plant and Phase will be there in the header section.
Fields details:

Water Consumed for Housing (M3)

Water Consumed Phase Utilities(M3)

Demin Water (M3)

 

DESAL ENTRY DAILY & OUTAGE HOURS
This is one of the major screens where business will record Production for MSF and MED units. The entry is at Unit level.
Plant and Phase will be there in the header section with date.

Fields details:

Online production – Production of the Multistage Flash (MSF) / Multiple Effect Distillation (MED).
Dump Production – Dump Production of the MSF/MED unit.
Reserve Production – Reserve Production of the MSF/MED unit.
Total Production - Auto Calculated (Online + Dump + Reserve)
Reason per min – The code for the less production. Predefined fields will be given, and business will select accordingly.


OUTAGE REASON SCREEN
This sub screen is needed to record the outage time and reason for the attached unit. Outage Reason screen will be added to all the production units.

              Available hours = Service Hours + Reserve Shutdown Hours
              Service Hours = Running hours of Unit with production
              Forced Outage Hours = Trip Hours + Forced S/D Hours
              Scheduled Outage = Planned Outage Hours + Maintenance Outage Hours + Overhaul Maintenance


RO ENTRY DAILY & OUTAGE HOURS
 Plant and Phase will be there in the header section with date.
Fields details:

On-line production – Production of the RO unit.
Dump Production – Dump Production of the RO unit.
Reserve Production – Reserve Production of the RO unit.
Total Production - Auto Calculated (Online + Dump + Reserve).


TURBINE ENTRY DAILY & OUTAGE HOURS
Plant and Phase will be there in the header section with date.
Fields details:

Gross Power - Power Generated for the TG
Auxiliary consumption - Power Consumption for BTG
Fuel Gas - Fuel Consumption of TG unit (Eastern Region)
Liquid Fuel - Fuel Consumed of TG in (Ton)
Diesel - Diesel Consumed entered in (M3) of TG


BOILER DATA ENTRY & OUTAGE HOURS
Plant and Phase will be there in the header section with date.
Fields details:

Steam Production – Produced steam of Boiler/HRSG.
Flue Gas Temperature – Temperature of the Fuel Gas of Boiler / HRSG
Combustion Air Flow -   Combustion air flow for Boiler/HRSG


DATA APPROVE (FOR PRODUCTION & PIPELINES)
Computed - Value which is entered in unit level and summed up at plant level.
Entered - Values entered in the plant screen.
City, quantity - Data entered in export water to cities daily against plant against each city.
Total Water Export - Data entered in export water to cities daily against plant.
Total Power Export - Data entered in Phase Screen for export Power against plant Summed up.
Phase Power Export - Data entered in Phase Screen for export Power against Phase
Phase Power Import - Data entered in Phase Screen for Import Power against Phase
Desal / Power / Boiler / RO - Data entered in unit level against the Phase selected.
MAN Production Planning (PP) 

Please follow my profile for future blogs related to SAP Production Planning.

Check out my LinkedIn Profile -> Abishek K G 

Let me know your views and suggestion to make this blog post better.
Thanks for taking your timeout to read this blog post.

Warm Regards,
Abishek K G

entry.title: Data Quality Management( DQM ) Derivation in SAP Master Data Central Governance
entry.content:
In this blog, our focus will be on the fundamental aspect of creating derivation rules for product master data. In this use case, we'll illustrate how to derive plants based on material type within an MDG Central environment using DQM derivation in S/4 2023 on-premises version.

Deriving plants based on material type is a common requirement in many organizations, especially in manufacturing or distribution industries where different types of materials may be associated with specific plant locations.

Before diving into the Use Case of DQM for SAP MDG Product or Material, preparing the Managing Derivation Scenarios tile, it's important to ensure that you have access to the necessary roles and authorization.

Role : SAP_BR_PRODMASTER_STEWARD

Authorization objects : 

 

MDC_DQEVALData Quality Evaluation Permissions (MDC_DQEVAL)
MDC_SIMULSimulation Permissions (MDC_SIMUL)
MDQRM_DS_EMaster Data Quality: Derivation Scenario Rule Execution (MDQRM_DS_E)
MDQRM_DS_RMaster Data Quality: Derivation Scenario Rule (MDQRM_DS_R)
MDQRM_DS_SMaster Data Quality: Derivation Scenario (MDQRM_DS_S)
MDQRM_DSEXMaster Data Quality: Derivation Scenario Export
MDQRM_DSIMMaster Data Quality: Derivation Scenario Import
MDQRM_MNDRMaster Data Quality: Mined Rule
MDQRM_MNNGMaster Data Quality: Rule Mining
MDQRM_RLEXMaster Data Quality: Validation Rule Export
MDQRM_RLIMMaster Data Quality: Validation Rule Import
MDQRM_RLUSMaster Data Quality: Validation Rule Usage
MDQRM_RULEMaster Data Quality: Validation Rule (MDQRM_RULE)
S_RFCAuthorization Check for RFC Access (S_RFC)

Authorization Objects Used by MDG, Data Quality Management | SAP Help Portal

 

Step 1: Create the Change Request Actions

  • Navigate to MDGIMG> General Settings > Process Modeling > Workflow > Define Change Request Actions

Action

Description

Pushbutton Text

Quick info Text

Check

Note

Reason

H1

DQM Derivation Successful

N/A

N/A

 

 

 

H2

DQM Derivation failed

N/A

N/A

 

 

 

H3

Continue

Continue

Continue

X

 

 

H4

Resubmit with DQM Derivation

Resubmit with DQM Derivation

Resubmit with DQM Derivation

X

 

 

H5

DQM Derivation Successful with Message

DQM Derivation Successful with Message

DQM Derivation Successful with Message

X

 

 

Why we need this action?

There are three possible outcomes when we integrate the DQM derivation with Central Governance,

  • Derivation Failed
  • Derivation Successful Without any message
  • Derivation Successful with Some message

Step 2: Define Change Request Step Types and Assign Actions

  • Navigate to General Settings > Process Modeling > Workflow > Define Change Request Step Types and Assign Actions
  • Create a new step type and assign the relevant actions created in step 1.
  • Ensure that dialog actions (e.g., continue or resubmit) are assigned for user interaction.

Create the New Step type “H” and assign the Actions Created in the above step 1, only assign the H3 and H4 , they are the dialog action , rest H1, H2 and H5 are background results.

Step 3: Configure Change Request Type

Note: The Integration of DQM Derivation is only possible with Rule Base Workflow

  • Navigate to MDGIMG > General Settings > Process Modeling > Change Request > Create Change Request Type.
  • Copy the standard CR type (e.g., MAT01) and create a new CR type (e.g., ZMATDQM) for integration with DQM derivation.

Step 4: Define Change Request Steps for Rule-Based Workflow

  • Navigate to MDGIMG > General Settings > Process Modeling > Workflow > Rule-Based Workflow > Define Change Request Steps for Rule-Based Workflow
  • Configure the steps for the rule-based workflow associated with the new CR type.
  • Define the conditions and corresponding actions based on DQM derivation outcomes.

Step 5: Define the Service Name

  • Navigate to MDGIMG > General Settings > Process Modeling > Workflow > Rule-Based Workflow > Define Service Names for Rule-Based Workflow
  • Define service names for the rule-based workflow “ZMDG_MM_DQM_DERIVATION”

Step 6: Configure the Rule Based Workflow

  • Navigate to MDGIMG > General Settings > Process Modeling > Workflow > Rule-Based Workflow > Configure Rule-Based Workflow
  • Configure the Rule Based workflow for the Change request type “ZMATDQM”

DT_SINGLE_VAL_ZMATDQM

CR Previous Step

Previous Action

Condition Alias

New Chng. Req. Step

New CR Status

=00

 

DQM

10

02

=10

=H1

DQS

20

02

=10

=H2

DQF

40

10

=10

=H5

DQW

40

10

=20

=06

REJ

92

03

=20

=05

ACT

90

02

=40

=H3

DQS

20

02

=40

=H4

DQM

10

02

=92

=07

DQS

20

02

=92

=08

ROL

99

06

=90

=31

END

99

05

=99

 

END

 

 

 

DT_USER_AGT_GRP_ZMATDQM

Condition Alias

User Agt Grp No.

Step Type

User Agent Type

User Agent Value

=DQS

1

3

AG

XXXXXXXXX

=DQF ; =DQW

1

H

AG

XXXXXXXXX

=REJ

1

4

SU

INIT

 

DT_NON_USER_AGT_GRP_ZMATDQM

Condition Alias

Agent Group

Process Pattern

Service Name

=DQM

1

02

ZMDG_MM_DQM_DERIVATION

=ACT

1

06

 

=ROL

1

08

 

=END

1

99

 

Step 7: Implement BAdI for Calling System Method

  • Navigate to MDGIMG > General Settings > Process Modeling > Workflow > Rule-Based Workflow > Business Add-Ins> BAdI: Calling of System Method for Rule-Based Workflow
  • Implement the BAdI "USMD_SSW_SYSTEM_METHOD_CALL" to call system methods for rule-based workflow.
  • Write code to execute DQM derivation and handle different derivation outcomes accordingly.

 

 

*Sample Code:
METHOD if_usmd_ssw_syst_method_caller~call_system_method. 
DATA(cr_number) = iv_cr_number. 
CASE iv_service_name. 
 WHEN ‘ZMDG_MM_DQM_DERIVATION’. 
"Get an instance of the MDQ derivation controller 
DATA(derivation_controller) = cl_mdg_mdq_rbwf_derive=>get_instance( ). 
"Execute the derivation and evaluate the result 
DATA(result) = derivation_controller->if_mdg_mdq_rbwf_derive~derive( change_request = cr_number ). 
IF result EQ derivation_controller->if_mdg_mdq_rbwf_derive~derivation_failure. 
"Indicate that the derivation failed  
ev_action = 'H2'. 
"INFO: access to messages via derivation_controller->messages 
ELSEIF result EQ derivation_controller->if_mdg_mdq_rbwf_derive~derivation_success_w_messages. 
"Indicate that the derivation was successful but with messages  
ev_action = 'H5'. 
"INFO: access to messages via derivation_controller->messages 
ELSE. 
"Indicate that the derivation was successful  
ev_action = 'H1'. 
ENDIF.           
WHEN OTHERS. 
 ENDCASE. 
ENDMETHOD.

 

 

Step 8: Define Derivation Scenarios

  • Open the "Define Derivation Scenarios" app to define derivation scenarios for products or materials.
  • Create a new scenario based on the requirement to derive material types based on certain conditions.

  • Click on the Create button to start defining the derivation from DQM.

  • Provide the following details, in this case we are trying to derive the material type based on the Material Type, so our base table shall be MARA.

  • Click on Create button to proceed further.

  • Click on Send for Implementation Button to implement the Scenario.

 

Step 9: Prepare Scope Expression and Define Derivation Rules

  • Prepare the scope expression for the CR type and navigate to the BRF Plus application to define the scope.
  • Define derivation rules using decision tables for conditions and results.
  • Specify conditions such as material types and corresponding plants to be derived.
  • Click on Prepare Scope Expression to prepare the scope, in our case the scope is for the CR type.

  • Click ok to proceed and later refresh to see the scope Expression, click on Scope Expression to proceed further, the navigation shall take to BRF Plus application , where we can define the scope of CR Type.

 

  • Set the scope and activate it and navigate back to Define Derivation

  • Click on Edit, Once edit mode is entered the scope which is initial in the below screen will be available in active state.

  • We have two sections for derivation, one is field and other is table, in this case we will use the table as we are going to derive the plant.

  • Provide the header, Rule details Section, rule Id and other related information as shown below.

  • In field section, provide the Condition field and Result fields, in this case conditional field is Material Type and Result Table is MARC and the field is plant and Click on Apply and then Save.

  • Select the Derivation Rules for table and set the status “To Be Implemented”. And Navigate to Derivation Rules for Tables

  • Click on Create Decision table and Click on Refresh button

  • Click on Condition Decision Table to maintain the condition and activate it.

  • Click on Result Decision Table and maintain all the plants which we want to be defaulted.

  • Once both Condition and Result Decision is active, the status changes to active.

Step 10: Review and Activate Derivation Rules

  • Review the derivation rules, ensuring correctness and completeness.
  • Activate the rules to make them available for use in the derivation process.
  • Set the status as “To be reviewed”

  • Set Status as “To be Approved”.

  • Set Status as “Approved”

  • Activate the Execution

Conclusion 

After completing all the configurations outlined above for derivation and integrating DQM (Data Quality Management) into the central governance Change Request (CR) process, the system will operate seamlessly to ensure data accuracy and consistency.

Once a user initiates a central governance CR, the DQM functionality, configured using BRF+ and the associated BADI's, will be automatically invoked in the background. This integration allows for the automatic validation and derivation of data based on predefined rules and logic.

If the DQM process succeeds, the CR will progress to the next step in the workflow, facilitating efficient data governance and approval processes. However, in case of any derivation failures, the system is designed to handle such scenarios effectively. The CR can be directed to the individual responsible for configuring the derivation rules, enabling them to review and rectify any issues. Alternatively, if necessary, the CR can be continued to the next step by bypassing the derivation process.

Ultimately, this setup ensures that data integrity is maintained throughout the central governance process, with automated checks and derivations occurring in the background to streamline operations and minimize manual intervention.

Hello,

Julian

entry.title: Multiple Employees who share the same Dependent
entry.content:
Solution:

It is currently not possible to assign the same dependent to two different employees via the User Interface; this must be done through the Import functionality instead. As a consequence, any changes brought to the custom fields of such dependents also need to be processed via Import.

In order to assign the same dependent to two different employees the following import files need to be used:

  • Biographical Info – use this file to create the Dependent that will be assigned to the employees.
    • In the person-id-external column, assign a suitable ID you can identify the Dependent by (this ID will be used in the next steps)
  • Consolidated Dependents – use this file to assign the previously created dependent to an employee and fill in any information the dependents require (eg. first name, last name, date of birth, citizenship, national ID, etc.)
  • Person Relationship – use this file to link the dependent to the other employee as well; kindly note that you need to fill in the custom fields with the same information as in the Consolidated Dependents file, otherwise those fields will show up as blank.
  • In the Consolidated Dependents in Person Relationship files:
    • the Related Person Id External should be the person-id-external assigned to the Dependent in the Biographical Information file
    • the Person ID External should be the ID of the User you are assigning the Dependent to

In cases where the same dependent is assigned to two Users the behavior for manual updates is the following:

  • updating dependents predefined fields (Biographical, Personal, National ID Information) will display the changes on both User records on the Employee Profile in the Dependents Section.
  • updating dependents custom fields will only display the changes on the Employee Profile the change was initiated on and will result in an error when editing the same dependent information on the second User.

The reason why the error in the screenshot is received is because a manual change was done to a custom field. After noticing that the change did not automatically propagate to the other record, the same change was manually performed on the 2nd record as well, which prompted the error.

A workaround for this is to use a different effective date to process the same change on the 2nd record. However, I do not recommend this as it is unclear what impact it might have in the long run. The recommendation would be to only update these cases via Import.

 

Summary:

  • If your client wants to process the changes manually, they can only do so for dependents Biographical Information, Personal Information or National ID fields. The information only needs to be changed on one record and will be reflected on the other as well.
  • If the change is for any of the custom fields, it will need to be done through import files. For this, only the Person Relationship file can be used. Kindly note that the same change will need to be done on both records, therefore include both employees that share a dependent in this file and fill in the custom fields with the dependent information for both of them. 

 

Notes:

  • the Person ID External should be the employee the dependent is being assigned to
  • the Related Person Id External should be the person-id-external set for the dependent in the previous file
  • the Consolidated Dependents Import works in Full Purge mode and is used to create the Dependent; every time this is imported it will overwrite for that Dependent so only use it once per dependent; don’t use this file to assign the dependent to both employees.

entry.title: Mapping of bank statement transaction codes is not working
entry.content:
Hello,

I have added a new mapping for MT940DE format and the external transaction code TAM is mapped to a payment method.
I also tried adding new rows with other transaction codes.
But every time i upload the bank statement, the system doesn't generate the configured payment methods and instead all the transactions are labeled as other outgoing and other incoming payments.
Why is the mapping not working?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Oil & Gas - Ultimate Data Security - Blockchain Data Backbone from OT to SAP IT🚀
entry.content:
The Problem

One of the Oil & Gas Industry's biggest challenges is protecting the data which is flowing from UpStream OT Operational Technology to SAP IT Information Technology.

Due to the very nature of Oil & Gas operations, more often than not, the OT Operational Technology is  located in geographically remote places, and a long way from the SAP Information Technology and DataCenter.

The OT Data is the most vulnerable, the most exposed to threats when it is flowing from the UpStream Sectore, the "E & P" Exploration and Production, to the SAP IT systems in the DataCenter:

SAP Oil Gas Operations Technology Data Risk Integration to S4HANA Industry Cloud atkrypto.io

 

The Threat:

The biggest threat to getting data from the Oil & Gas Operational Technology to the SAP Information Technology, is the threat of Cyber Attacks. The attack vector at most risk is the movement of the data, or the data on the move, the integration between the OT and the SAP IT.

In their March 2023 Report, "How to enhance the cybersecurity of operational technology environments", McKinsey and Company highlight the need for "strengthening technological foundations", and in particular, highlighting that,

"integration between OT systems and ERP systems increase the need for secure convergence between the IT and OT environments"

 Courtney Schneider, in the report, "OT security incidents in 2021: Trends & Analysis," Waterfall Security Solutions, May 17, 2022, noted that,

"OT cyberattacks tend to have higher, more negative effects than those in IT do, as they can have physical consequences (for example, shutdowns, outages, leakages, and explosions). Of 64 OT cyberattacks publicly reported in 2021 (an increase of 140 percent over the number reported in 2020), approximately 35 percent had physical consequences, and the estimated damages were $140 million per incident."

 

Today's Legacy Approach to Integration Security

Data Security has evolved slowly during that last 30 years:

Evolution of Data Security atkrypto.io

Data Encryption is not enough

Encryption is not enough atkrypto.io

 

The Business Demand

The Oil & Gas industry is demanding more, the Business is demanding better security of integration between OT and SAP IT.

 

The Digital Transformation of Information Security is Enterprise Blockchain, The Next Generation Data Integrity, Originality, Confidentiality Protection

Enterprise Blockchain, Enterprise Distributed Ledger Technology is re-imagining information security.

Enterprise Blockchain Platforms bring, out of the box, natively, a level of security which is not possible out of the box, natively, with any other commercial database product.

What is so special about the Enterprise Blockchain, (and discussed in the previous blogs, here, here, and here) is that Blockchain Distributed Ledger Technology has four special characteristics which make it information security re-imagined, and the next generation of information security, these special characteristics are: 

atkrypto.io what is a blockchain SAP Oil Gas OT IT

McKinsey & Company, in their December 2023 Featured Insights Publication, gave a beautiful description of what is unique and special about Blockchain, "Blockchain is a secure database shared across a network of participants, where up-to-date information is available to all participants at the same time". If we just pause for a moment and let that sink in, and think about what that means, to Business Processes, to Collaboration, to System Resilience, we start to see what is so special about Blockchain Databases and Distributed Ledger Technology.

This means, we can have the an Enterprise Blockchain Database Tenant running at the Oil & Gas E&P OT sector and an Enterprise Blockchain Database Tenant running at the SAP DataCenter location, and the same data available in both places and protected to the highest level by the Blockchain's Hash Mechanism, Consensus Mechanism, Immutable status, and Distributed resilience.

We can now run Enterprise Blockchain Database from the Exploration and Production sector to the SAP system in the DataCenter, protecting the OT Data as it flows to the SAP IT, to a level of security protection that was not possible with the previous generation of technology:

Blockchain Next Generation Data Protection SAP Oil Gas OT IT atkrypto.io

 

Wrapping Up and Reference Architecture

To wrap up, a simple reminder, 

The Digital Transformation of Information Security is Enterprise Blockchain

Enterprise Blockchain is the Next Generation Data Integrity, Originality, Confidentiality Protection

Enterprise Blockchain, Enterprise Distributed Ledger Technology is re-imagining information security.

SAP have got everything in place for you to do this today, and here's the thing, and now, within the SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem there are enabling technology Blockchain Products designed and built by SAP Experts specifically for the needs of SAP Customers to make doing Blockchain and SAP easy, and so today, you can do SAP and Blockchain, it's no longer hype, today it's real and there's nothing stopping you.

 

Reference Architecture Event Driven Blockchain with SAP

Here is an example of how Enterprise Blockchain can be implemented to bring next generation data integrity protection to the Oil and Gas OT to IT integrations.

The Enterprise Blockchain Database Tenant is running at the E&P Sector and at the same time, the Enterprise Blockchain Database Tenant is running at the SAP Business Technology Platform. The OT Data is written to the Enterprise Blockchain Database and the SAP S/4HANA and or SAP Industry Cloud read the data from the same Enterprise Blockchain Database. 

SAP Oil and Gas OT to IT Integration Blockchain Security atkrypto.io

As McKinsey & Company, in their December 2023 Featured Insights Publication, gave a beautiful description of what is unique and special about Blockchain, "Blockchain is a secure database shared across a network of participants, where up-to-date information is available to all participants at the same time".

The good news is, as we discussed in the previous blog, this is no longer hype, we can do all of this today, and now, within the SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem there are enabling technology Blockchain Products designed and built by SAP Experts specifically for the needs of SAP Customers to make doing Blockchain and SAP easy, and so you can do SAP and Blockchain, today it's real and there's nothing stopping you.

So what are we waiting for ? Oh yeah, more use cases, ok, that will continue in  the next blog

What do you think, are the words Blockchain, Web3, Distributed Ledger Technology, starting to appear in your Company's visions and technology visions ? What use cases are you looking at ? Let's chat about it in the comments.

For now, over and out.

Andy Silvey.

Independent SAP Technical Architect and CEO of atkrypto.io

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

Andy is also co-Founder of atkrypto inc, an startup whose ambition is to make Blockchain easy for Enterprise.

atkrypto.io's flagship product is the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP,  and atkrypto.io is a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner. 

The atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has been designed by SAP Independent Experts for the needs of SAP Customers and to be deployed on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Service and leverage native integration to SAP Products.

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has a number of unique qualities, including being the only Blockchain software in the world which has a DataCenter version and a light mobile version which can run on Edge/IoT/Mobile devices and enables data to be written to the Blockchain at the Edge where that same Blockchain is running on a Server in the DataCenter, protecting the integrity and originality of data from the Edge to Insights. Taking Blockchain to the Data at the Edge instead of taking the Data to the Blockchain.
